# (APPENDIX) Appendix {-}

# Bibliography {#bibliography}

## Books

[**Advanced High School Statistics 3rd Ed**](https://www.openintro.org/book/ahss/), Diez et al: A detailed and rigorous introduction to statistics that would suit advanced high school and undergraduate university students. The theory and applications of statistics are covered from collecting data through to inference and statistical tests. The book contains numerous exercise and examples to help students develop their knowledge and skills.

[**Introductory Statistics**](https://stats.libretexts.org/Bookshelves/Introductory_Statistics/Introductory_Statistics_(Shafer_and_Zhang)), Shafer and Zhang: Hosted on [LibreTexts](https://libretexts.org/), this is another thorough but accessible introduction to the theory and practice of statistics. The book begins with a detailed explanation of descriptive statistics and probability, and random variables. Later chapters cover sampling distributions, hypotheses, and hypothesis testing.

[**Learning Statistics with R - A tutorial for Psychology Students and other Beginners**](https://stats.libretexts.org/Bookshelves/Applied_Statistics/Learning_Statistics_with_R_-_A_tutorial_for_Psychology_Students_and_other_Beginners_(Navarro)), Navarro, D: An example-based introduction to statistics using R. Includes data sets and a custom R package (lsr).

## Websites

[**Statistical Terms and Concepts**](https://www.abs.gov.au/statistics/understanding-statistics/statistical-terms-and-concepts), Australian Bureau of Statistics: A comprehensive glossary of statistical terms. Click the terms for detailed explanations.

# Online Tools

[**Statistics Kingdom**](https://www.statskingdom.com/index.html): Perform statistical tasks including plotting and statistical tests online. The home page includes an extensive summary of statistical tests, their use, requirements, and assumptions. There is also a handy [Statistical Test Finder](https://www.statskingdom.com/statistical-decision-tree.html) that will help you select an appropriate test for your purpose.

[**HHMI Data Explorer**](https://media.hhmi.org/biointeractive/tool/dataexplorer/v1/#/home): Create interactive plots and perform statistical tests online.


# Statistical Information In Scientific Reports

## APA 7th

[APA 7th](https://apastyle.apa.org/) referencing and styles are widely used for scientific reports. The APA 7th styles include guidelines about

* [Numbers and Statistics](https://apastyle.apa.org/instructional-aids/numbers-statistics-guide.pdf)
* [Tables](https://apastyle.apa.org/style-grammar-guidelines/tables-figures/tables)
* [Figures](https://apastyle.apa.org/style-grammar-guidelines/tables-figures/figures)


## Reporting Statistical Test Results

Here is an example from Navarro ([12.1.9 report the results of the test](https://stats.libretexts.org/Bookshelves/Applied_Statistics/Learning_Statistics_with_R_-_A_tutorial_for_Psychology_Students_and_other_Beginners_(Navarro)/12%3A_Categorical_Data_Analysis/12.01%3A_The_2_Goodness-of-fit_Test)) that demonstrates a comprehensive but concise way to report the results of a statistical test.

"Of the 200 participants in the experiment, 64 selected hearts for their first choice, 51 selected diamonds, 50 selected spades, and 35 selected clubs. A chi-square goodness of fit test was conducted to test whether the choice probabilities were identical for all four suits. The results were significant (χ2(3)=8.44,p<.05), suggesting that people did not select suits purely at random."

Note that,

* the descriptive statistics are given first
* the type of test is named explicitly 
* the null hypothesis is given
* the result of the test is given in words followed by the statistical output of the test in brackets
* lastly, the results are interpreted.
